title: verilog: fix buf/not primitives with multiple outputs
body:
From IEEE1364-2005 section 7.3, buf and not gates:

> These two logic gates shall have one input and one or more outputs. The last terminal in the terminal list shall connect to the input of the logic gate, and the other terminals shall connect to the outputs of the logic gate.

yosys does not follow this and instead interprets the first argument as the output, the second as the input and ignores the rest.

There exists a test in `tests/asicworld/code_verilog_tutorial_n_out_primitive.v` that should probably catch this; not sure what's up with that.
